goal: Land the foundational embedding chunker in `crates/ucil-embeddings/` per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786 (the W8 embedding pipeline) AND §12.2 line 1339 (`Chunking`: AST-aware via tree-sitter. Each chunk is a complete function/method/class. Never split mid-function. Max 512 tokens. Larger functions: signature + first-paragraph doc comment.). The feature description (`feature-list.json:P2-W8-F05`) reads: 'Embedding chunker: splits source files at tree-sitter function/class boundaries into chunks of ≤512 tokens; preserves file path and line-range metadata.' This is the REAL-tokenizer chunker explicitly anticipated by `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/chunker.rs:56-58` ('the real tokenizer is owned by `ucil-embeddings` ... A byte-based estimate is sufficient to enforce the 'no chunk larger than 512 tokens' invariant at this layer — any minor drift between the estimate and a real tokenizer is absorbed by the downstream embedder's own chunk-boundary-respecting tokenizer.'). Create `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` (NEW module) declaring `pub struct EmbeddingChunker` whose `chunk(file_path: &Path, source: &str, language: ucil_treesitter::Language) -> Result<Vec<EmbeddingChunk>, EmbeddingChunkerError>` (1) parses the source via `ucil_treesitter::Parser`, (2) emits AST-aware chunks via `ucil_treesitter::Chunker::chunk` (byte-estimated per P1-W2-F03), (3) RE-TOKENIZES each chunk's content via the real `tokenizers::Tokenizer` (HuggingFace BPE), (4) for any chunk whose REAL-tokenizer count > 512, applies the master-plan §12.2 line 1339 oversize policy (signature + first-paragraph doc comment, mirroring `ucil-treesitter::chunker`'s shape), (5) returns `Vec<EmbeddingChunk { file_path, start_line, end_line, content, token_count }>` where `token_count` is the REAL tokenizer count (NOT the byte-estimate). The frozen acceptance test `chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture` lands at MODULE ROOT of `chunker.rs` per DEC-0007, exercising chunking against a small bundled fixture `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/sample.rs` with a synthetic in-test tokenizer (built programmatically via `tokenizers::Tokenizer::new` + `tokenizers::models::wordlevel::WordLevel` — zero file dependency). This WO unblocks P2-W8-F04 (LanceDB background indexing) which consumes chunks as the embedding-input stream, and P2-W8-F08 (find_similar MCP tool) which uses chunked snippets at query time.
plan_summary: Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` (~250-450 LOC including module preamble + error enum + struct + impl + 1 frozen acceptance test + 4-6 unit tests). Add `ucil-treesitter = { path = "../ucil-treesitter" }` to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion before `ucil-core`). The `EmbeddingChunker` struct stores a `tokenizers::Tokenizer` (owned) plus a `ucil_treesitter::Parser` (owned, mutable per its API). Constructor split into TWO factories per the WO-0059-extracted-helper-for-testability lesson: `pub fn from_tokenizer_path(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, EmbeddingChunkerError>` (production path — loads HF tokenizer.json from disk) and `pub fn from_tokenizer(tokenizer: Tokenizer) -> Self` (synthetic-tokenizer injection for tests; constructs with a fresh `Parser`). The `chunk()` method (1) calls `self.parser.parse(source, language)` to get a `tree_sitter::Tree`, (2) calls `ucil_treesitter::Chunker::new().chunk(&tree, source, file_path, language)` to get AST-aware boundary chunks, (3) for each AST chunk: tokenize via `self.tokenizer.encode(content, false)?` (returns Encoding with `.get_ids().len()` = real token count); IF `real_count <= 512`: emit `EmbeddingChunk { file_path, start_line, end_line, content, token_count: real_count }`; IF `real_count > 512`: build a signature-only fallback (first non-blank line of content + first paragraph of doc-comment if available via `ts_chunk.symbol_name`'s extracted-symbol metadata — but `Chunk` doesn't expose `signature` directly, so we use the first 2-3 non-blank lines of `content` as the signature heuristic), re-tokenize the signature, emit with truncated `token_count`. (4) Return `Vec<EmbeddingChunk>` in source order. `EmbeddingChunkerError` is `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive]` with variants: `Parse { source: ucil_treesitter::ParseError }`, `Chunk { source: ucil_treesitter::ChunkError }`, `Tokenizer { message: String }`, `MissingTokenizerFile { path: PathBuf }`, `EncodingFailure { content_len: usize, message: String }`. Update `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs`: add `pub mod chunker;` and `pub use chunker::{EmbeddingChunk, EmbeddingChunker, EmbeddingChunkerError, MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS};` per the WO-0042→WO-0058 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 13 consecutive cleared). Add `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/sample.rs` — a small Rust source fixture (~30-60 lines, 3-5 functions) that produces ≥3 chunks and stays under the synthetic-tokenizer's 512-token cap on each function. NO new top-level workspace dep is added (ucil-treesitter is a path dep already declared in `Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]`; tokenizers + thiserror + ucil-core are already in `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml`). Pre-bake 3 mutation entries (literal sed AND runtime-only variants per WO-0046 lessons): (M1) `chunk()` body neutered to early `Ok(Vec::new())` → frozen test panics on chunk-count assertion; (M2) the oversize-fallback branch neutered to skip splitting (let oversize chunks through unchanged) → oversize-test panics on the `token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` invariant; (M3) `EmbeddingChunk.token_count` field assignment swapped from `real_count` to AST byte-estimate (i.e. `ts_chunk.token_count`) → frozen test panics because the synthetic tokenizer's count materially differs from the byte-estimate (one-token-per-word vs one-token-per-4-bytes). Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F05.sh` (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` post-WO-0058 and `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` post-WO-0059). Pin coverage gate `bash scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh ucil-embeddings 85 75` directly in WO acceptance per WO-0059 lessons line 606 — ALL negative-error-paths get unit-test coverage so coverage stays ≥85% line floor.
scope_in: Add `ucil-treesitter = { path = "../ucil-treesitter" }` to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]` immediately BEFORE the existing `ucil-core = { path = "../ucil-core" }` line so the alphabetical-within-block ordering holds (`ucil-core` < `ucil-treesitter`). NO new top-level `Cargo.toml` workspace dep is added — `ucil-treesitter` is a path dep on a sibling crate. AC02 verifies via `grep -nE '^ucil-treesitter = \{ path = "\.\./ucil-treesitter" \}' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` returning exactly 1 match.

Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` (~250-450 LOC). Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble cites: (a) master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786 (W8 embedding pipeline), (b) master-plan §12.2 line 1339 verbatim (the chunking contract), (c) the role of `EmbeddingChunker` as the REAL-tokenizer chunker downstream of `ucil-treesitter::chunker` (which uses byte-estimates); cross-reference `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/chunker.rs:56-58` explicitly, (d) the deliberate dual-constructor surface (`from_tokenizer_path` for production, `from_tokenizer` for testing — extract-pure-helper-for-testability per WO-0059 lessons line 609), (e) the deferral of consumer wiring to F04 (LanceDB indexer) and F08 (find_similar MCP tool). Backtick every uppercase identifier in rustdoc paragraphs per WO-0043 lessons line 128 (`` `EmbeddingChunker` ``, `` `Tokenizer` ``, `` `HuggingFace` ``, `` `BPE` ``, `` `MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` ``, etc.). Pre-flight: `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` returns ZERO unbacked uppercase tokens.

Declare `pub const MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS: u32 = 512;` at module-root, with rustdoc citing master-plan §12.4 line 2030 (`chunk_max_tokens = 512`). This is the same value `ucil_treesitter::MAX_TOKENS` exposes; the constant is duplicated here intentionally because `ucil-embeddings` is the layer where the REAL-tokenizer cap is enforced (any drift from the byte-estimate is absorbed at this layer per the master-plan + ucil-treesitter docstring rationale).

Declare `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum EmbeddingChunkerError` with variants: (a) `#[error("tree-sitter parse failed: {source}")] Parse { #[from] source: ucil_treesitter::ParseError }`, (b) `#[error("tree-sitter chunking failed: {source}")] Chunk { #[from] source: ucil_treesitter::ChunkError }`, (c) `#[error("tokenizer load/decode failed: {message}")] Tokenizer { message: String }` (the `tokenizers::Error` type does not implement `std::error::Error`-with-`From`-friendly conversion in the workspace's pinned version per WO-0059 retry-1 line 272 precedent — wrap as `format!("{e}")`), (d) `#[error("tokenizer file does not exist: {path:?}")] MissingTokenizerFile { path: PathBuf }` (early existence check before `Tokenizer::from_file`), (e) `#[error("failed to encode chunk content (len={content_len}): {message}")] EncodingFailure { content_len: usize, message: String }` (carry the offending content length in the panic message — operator-actionable diagnostic per WO-0051 lessons line 405). The `#[non_exhaustive]` attribute follows `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` libraries-use-thiserror invariant.

Declare `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct EmbeddingChunk` with fields: `pub file_path: PathBuf`, `pub start_line: u32`, `pub end_line: u32`, `pub content: String`, `pub token_count: u32`. Rustdoc cites master-plan §12.2 line 1324-1336 (`code_chunks_schema`) and notes that `EmbeddingChunk` is the embedding-tokenizer-aware projection of that schema (no `id` / `language` / `symbol_name` / `symbol_kind` / `embedding` / `file_hash` / `indexed_at` — those are populated downstream at the LanceDB-write layer in F04). The struct does NOT derive `Hash` (unnecessary today; add later if F04/F08 need it).

Declare `#[derive(Debug)] pub struct EmbeddingChunker { tokenizer: tokenizers::Tokenizer, parser: ucil_treesitter::Parser }`. NOT `Clone` (`Tokenizer` may not be cheaply clonable; consumers needing shared ownership wrap in `Arc<Mutex<EmbeddingChunker>>`). The struct holds `&mut self`-requiring resources — `chunk()` takes `&mut self` because `Parser::parse` likely takes `&mut self` (verify against `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` Parser API during executor research; if `Parser::parse` is `&self`, then `chunk()` may be `&self` too). Per WO-0058 lessons line 561 (ort `&mut self` precedent): consumers requiring shared inference wrap in `Arc<Mutex<...>>`.

Implement `pub fn from_tokenizer(tokenizer: tokenizers::Tokenizer) -> Self` constructor — direct injection for testing. Body: `Self { tokenizer, parser: ucil_treesitter::Parser::new() }`. Rustdoc cites WO-0059 lessons line 609 (extract-pure-helper-for-testability) and notes this is the test-injection seam per DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait/struct boundaries; synthetic-tokenizer injection in tests is NOT a critical-dep mock — `tokenizers::Tokenizer` is constructed via the real `tokenizers` crate API, just with a synthetic vocab).

Implement `pub fn from_tokenizer_path(tokenizer_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, EmbeddingChunkerError>` — production path. Body: (1) early existence check `if !tokenizer_path.exists() { return Err(EmbeddingChunkerError::MissingTokenizerFile { path: tokenizer_path.to_path_buf() }); }`, (2) `let tokenizer = tokenizers::Tokenizer::from_file(tokenizer_path).map_err(|e| EmbeddingChunkerError::Tokenizer { message: format!("{e}") })?;`, (3) `Ok(Self::from_tokenizer(tokenizer))`. Tracing: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.chunker.load", level = "debug", skip(tokenizer_path), fields(path = ?tokenizer_path))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>`.

Implement `pub fn chunk(&mut self, file_path: &Path, source: &str, language: ucil_treesitter::Language) -> Result<Vec<EmbeddingChunk>, EmbeddingChunkerError>` with this contract: (1) parse the source via `let tree = self.parser.parse(source, language)?;` (the `?` converts via `From<ucil_treesitter::ParseError>`); if `parse` returns `Option<Tree>` rather than `Result`, treat `None` as a `Parse { source: <synthesised error> }` — verify the actual `Parser::parse` signature during executor research and document any `Option`→`Result` adapter inline. (2) Build the AST chunks: `let ast_chunks = ucil_treesitter::Chunker::new().chunk(&tree, source, file_path, language)?;`. (3) For each `ast_chunk` in `ast_chunks`, invoke a private helper `fn retokenize_chunk(&self, ast_chunk: &ucil_treesitter::Chunk) -> Result<EmbeddingChunk, EmbeddingChunkerError>` (extracted-pure-helper for unit testability per WO-0059 lessons line 600). The helper: (a) tokenizes content via `let encoding = self.tokenizer.encode(ast_chunk.content.as_str(), false).map_err(|e| EmbeddingChunkerError::EncodingFailure { content_len: ast_chunk.content.len(), message: format!("{e}") })?;`, (b) `let real_count = u32::try_from(encoding.get_ids().len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX);`, (c) IF `real_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS`: emit `EmbeddingChunk { file_path: ast_chunk.file_path.clone(), start_line: ast_chunk.start_line, end_line: ast_chunk.end_line, content: ast_chunk.content.clone(), token_count: real_count }`; (d) IF `real_count > MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS`: invoke a private `fn collapse_to_signature(&self, ast_chunk: &ucil_treesitter::Chunk) -> Result<EmbeddingChunk, EmbeddingChunkerError>` helper that takes the first ≤3 non-blank lines of `ast_chunk.content` as the signature (master-plan §12.2 line 1339 says 'signature + first-paragraph doc comment'; the `ucil_treesitter::Chunk` struct doesn't surface `signature` separately so the heuristic is the first non-blank lines), re-tokenizes the signature, and emits an `EmbeddingChunk` with the same `start_line` / `end_line` / `file_path` and the signature `content` + signature `token_count`. The signature MUST satisfy `signature_token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS`; if not (rare — a 512+-token signature line), hard-truncate to the first byte-budget that satisfies the cap. (4) Collect all `EmbeddingChunk`s (one per AST chunk, with oversize ones collapsed) and return `Ok(chunks)`. Tracing on `chunk()`: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.chunker.chunk", level = "debug", skip(self, source), fields(file = %file_path.display(), language = ?language, source_len = source.len()))]`.

Add the FROZEN acceptance test `#[test] fn test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The test selector `chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture` matches the frozen `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F05.acceptance_tests[0].selector = '-p ucil-embeddings chunker::'` substring. Test body (5 sub-assertions in order):  
  **Setup**: build a synthetic tokenizer in-test via `let tokenizer = build_synthetic_tokenizer();` where `build_synthetic_tokenizer()` is a private `#[cfg(test)]` helper at module root that constructs `tokenizers::Tokenizer::new(WordLevelBuilder::new().build().unwrap())` with a `Whitespace` pre-tokenizer (one token per whitespace-separated word — UNK token used for every word since the vocab is empty). This produces deterministic token counts on real source text.  
  **Setup**: locate the bundled fixture: `let manifest_dir = PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")); let fixture_path = manifest_dir.join("tests").join("data").join("sample.rs"); let source = std::fs::read_to_string(&fixture_path).expect("sample.rs fixture present");` (per WO-0058 lessons line 545 — `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` is the canonical fixture-path resolution).  
  **Action**: `let mut chunker = EmbeddingChunker::from_tokenizer(tokenizer); let chunks = chunker.chunk(&fixture_path, &source, ucil_treesitter::Language::Rust).expect("chunk over sample.rs");`.  
  **(SA1) Chunk count is positive**: `assert!(!chunks.is_empty(), "sample.rs must produce at least one chunk; got {:?}", chunks);`  
  **(SA2) Every chunk respects the cap**: `for chunk in &chunks { assert!(chunk.token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS, "chunk {:?} violates token cap (token_count={}, MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS={})", chunk, chunk.token_count, MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS); }`. Operator-readable panic per WO-0051 lessons line 405.  
  **(SA3) Token count uses REAL tokenizer (not byte-estimate)**: pick the chunk whose content has a known whitespace-word count from sample.rs (e.g. the first chunk's `content` is the first function body — the synthetic tokenizer counts words). Assert `chunks[0].token_count == <expected_word_count>`. The byte-estimate (`max(1, ⌈len/4⌉)`) materially differs from the word count for typical Rust source (~5 chars per word average), so this assertion catches a regression where the chunker copies the byte-estimate instead of using the real tokenizer.  
  **(SA4) Metadata preserved**: `chunks[0].file_path == fixture_path` AND `chunks[0].start_line >= 1` AND `chunks[0].end_line >= chunks[0].start_line` AND `!chunks[0].content.is_empty()`. Each assertion uses operator-readable messages: `assert_eq!(chunks[0].file_path, fixture_path, "file_path metadata must round-trip; got {:?}", chunks[0].file_path)` etc.  
  **(SA5) Source-order ordering**: `for window in chunks.windows(2) { assert!(window[0].start_line <= window[1].start_line, "chunks must be in source order; got {:?} then {:?}", window[0], window[1]); }` — proves the chunker preserves AST traversal order from `ucil_treesitter::Chunker`.

Add unit tests at file-end inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; ... }` per WO-0059 lessons line 601 (preserve module-root frozen-selector + add coverage-driving negative-path tests). Required tests:  
  (a) `from_tokenizer_path_returns_missing_tokenizer_file_for_absent_path` — pass a non-existent path, assert `Err(MissingTokenizerFile { path })` matching the expected path.  
  (b) `from_tokenizer_path_returns_tokenizer_for_invalid_json` — write 21 bytes of garbage to a `tempfile::NamedTempFile` with `.json` extension, assert `Err(Tokenizer { message })` (the `Tokenizer::from_file` parse fails). Mirrors WO-0059 retry-1's `tokenizer_absent` shape with the lesson learned: name the test for what it actually tests (`tokenizer_for_invalid_json`, NOT `tokenizer_for_missing_file` — see WO-0059 anti-pattern line 616).  
  (c) `chunk_returns_parse_error_for_invalid_source` — the executor researches whether `ucil_treesitter::Parser::parse` returns `ParseError` on invalid source (it may successfully parse anything tree-sitter can fit into an error tree); if `parse` is infallible on the test input, document inline + skip this test rather than synthesise a fake error path.  
  (d) `retokenize_chunk_collapses_oversize_to_signature` — construct a synthetic `ucil_treesitter::Chunk` literal with content of 1000+ whitespace-separated words (forces `real_count > 512`), invoke `chunker.retokenize_chunk(&fake_chunk)`, assert returned `EmbeddingChunk.token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS`. (Make `retokenize_chunk` `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)` to enable in-test invocation; document the visibility promotion in the rustdoc per WO-0055 lessons line 457.)  
  (e) `collapse_to_signature_handles_single_line_oversize_content` — build a synthetic 5000-character single-line content (no `\n`), pass through `collapse_to_signature`, assert `token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` AND `content.len() <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS * 4` (byte-budget hard-truncation worked).  
  (f) `embedding_chunk_round_trips_metadata` — construct an `EmbeddingChunk` literal, assert each public field is accessible. Smoke test for the `Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq` derive set.

Add `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/sample.rs` — a small Rust source fixture, ~30-60 lines, containing 3-5 functions. Each function body is short (≤ 40 whitespace-separated words) so the synthetic tokenizer's count stays under MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS for every function. Example shape: a module-level doc comment + `fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { a + b }` + `fn multiply(...)` + `fn divide(...)` + `struct Calculator { ... }` + `impl Calculator { fn new() -> Self {...} fn compute(...) {...} }`. The fixture is REAL Rust syntax that tree-sitter-rust can parse cleanly (no syntax errors). Add a leading `//` comment block citing WO-0060 + P2-W8-F05 + the synthetic-tokenizer test contract so a future maintainer doesn't refactor it out (the file has no production consumer; it's purely a chunker-test fixture).

Update `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs`: (a) add `pub mod chunker;` immediately after the existing `pub mod onnx_inference;` line so the module is exported (alphabetical: `chunker` < `models` < `onnx_inference`, but the existing order is `models` < `onnx_inference` — the executor decides whether to MAINTAIN the existing order with `chunker` inserted alphabetically OR follow the existing pattern; pick alphabetical and adjust the existing order if needed in a SEPARATE commit). (b) add `pub use chunker::{EmbeddingChunk, EmbeddingChunker, EmbeddingChunkerError, MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS};` immediately after the `pub use models::...` line. Use `#[rustfmt::skip]` if the line exceeds rustfmt's 100-col wrap budget per WO-0056 lessons line 490. (c) extend the existing module-level `//!` preamble (line 1-21) with one trailing paragraph describing the `EmbeddingChunker` surface + the F04/F08 deferral pattern (consumers in subsequent WOs) + master-plan §12.2 line 1339 citation. Verify post-edit via `grep -nE 'pub mod chunker;|pub use chunker::' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs | wc -l` returning `2`. Cumulative re-export-discipline streak: 13 consecutive WOs cleared (WO-0042 → WO-0058, WO-0059); this WO maintains it.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F05.sh` per the standing convention (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` post-WO-0058 + `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` post-WO-0059). Script must: (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings chunker:: -- --nocapture` and tee stdout to `/tmp/test-WO-0060.log`; (b) match `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests passed` shape via the tolerant alternation `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'` (per WO-0042 / WO-0048 / WO-0051 / WO-0055 / WO-0056 / WO-0058 lessons); (c) on non-zero exit, emit `[FAIL] P2-W8-F05: <reason>` with hints pointing at `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/sample.rs` + the WO + master-plan §12.2 line 1339; (d) include the `shellcheck` PATH-fallback pattern (`if command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then shellcheck "$0"; else echo "[INFO] shellcheck not on PATH; skipping shellcheck step."; fi`); (e) `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W8-F05.sh` so the verifier can run it directly. Header comment cites WO-0060 + P2-W8-F05 + the test selector verbatim.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 + WO-0056 AC17 + WO-0058 AC15/AC16 — for `chunk()` mutation (M1: empty Vec return), the body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables, unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return)] { let _ = (file_path, source, language); return Ok(Vec::new()); }`. Document BOTH the literal sed AND runtime-only variants in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC22 / AC23 / AC24).

**Pre-flight rustdoc grep before pushing** (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown` + WO-0058 AC02): `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` returns ZERO unbacked uppercase tokens relative to `git show main:crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs | rg -cE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b'` baseline. Every uppercase identifier in rustdoc is in backticks (`` `EmbeddingChunker` ``, `` `Tokenizer` ``, `` `MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` ``, `` `BPE` ``, `` `WordLevel` ``, `` `Whitespace` ``, etc.).

**Operator-readable assertion messages** (per WO-0051 lessons line 405 + WO-0055 line 440 + WO-0058 lessons): every `assert_eq!` / `assert!` in `test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture` AND every unit test follows `assert!(<predicate>, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", <actual_value>)` so a verifier failure prints the actual observed value, not just the line number.

**Workspace-build precondition** (per WO-0055 line 456 + WO-0056 line 513 + WO-0058 standing rule): the executor MUST run `cargo build --workspace --tests` exiting 0 BEFORE writing the ready-for-review marker. Per-crate `cargo check -p ucil-embeddings` is NOT sufficient because lib.rs re-export changes are visible to every downstream crate.

**Pre-flight commit-subject length validation** (per WO-0059 lessons line 607): every prescribed commit subject MUST be ≤70 characters. The executor MAY trim subjects locally if a draft exceeds 70 chars, BUT MUST NOT use `git commit --amend` after push. Recommended subjects (each ≤70 chars):  
  (1) `build(embeddings): add ucil-treesitter path dep for chunker module` (62 chars)  
  (2) `feat(embeddings): add EmbeddingChunker skeleton + error enum + types` (68 chars)  
  (3) `feat(embeddings): implement chunk() with retokenize and oversize fallback` (DROP — 73 chars; replace with `feat(embeddings): implement chunk() with HF retokenization` at 56 chars)  
  (4) `test(embeddings): add chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture` (64 chars)  
  (5) `test(embeddings): add chunker negative-path unit tests for coverage` (65 chars)  
  (6) `feat(embeddings): wire chunker into lib.rs + add P2-W8-F05.sh` (60 chars)  
  Each commit's body cites `Phase: 2 / Feature: P2-W8-F05 / Work-order: WO-0060` per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`.

**Coverage-floor pre-flight** (per WO-0059 lessons line 606 — implicit coverage-gate caused retry-0 rejection). The new `chunker.rs` module has 5 thiserror variants — every error variant MUST be reachable from at least one unit test in `mod tests {}`. Pre-bake: variant (a) `Parse` reachable from test (c) `chunk_returns_parse_error_for_invalid_source` IF parse is fallible; ELSE document the unreachability inline and accept the consequence on `Parse` line coverage. variant (b) `Chunk` — exercised transitively when `ucil_treesitter::Chunker::chunk` returns Err on malformed AST line ranges (pre-bake test `chunk_propagates_treesitter_chunk_error` if needed; otherwise document gap). variant (c) `Tokenizer` reachable from test (b) `from_tokenizer_path_returns_tokenizer_for_invalid_json`. variant (d) `MissingTokenizerFile` reachable from test (a) `from_tokenizer_path_returns_missing_tokenizer_file_for_absent_path`. variant (e) `EncodingFailure` — typically unreachable in practice (well-formed UTF-8 always encodes); pre-bake or document gap. Target: ≥85% line coverage on `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` measured by `bash scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh ucil-embeddings 85 75`. AC30 explicitly pins this gate.

**Commit ladder — 6 commits** per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` and DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out. If commit-#2 (skeleton + error enum + types) stretches to ~250-450 LOC due to module-coherence requirements (intermediate `unused_*` warnings under `#![deny(warnings)]` would block any narrower split), cite DEC-0005 + WO-0048 297-LOC framing precedent + WO-0056 542-LOC NEW-module precedent + WO-0058 305-LOC precedent inline in the commit body per WO-0056 lessons line 502.
scope_out: **No CodeRankEmbed model integration.** F02 (P2-W8-F02 / WO-0059) ships `CodeRankEmbed::embed`; F05 trusts callers to provide a `Tokenizer` (production callers in F04 / F08 will wire `CodeRankEmbed`'s tokenizer or load `tokenizer.json` directly). NO download/load of `ml/models/coderankembed/model.onnx` in this WO; NO call to `CodeRankEmbed::load` from the chunker code path.

**No batched-input or multi-file optimisation.** `EmbeddingChunker::chunk` processes ONE source file at a time. Batching across files (for LanceDB indexer's bulk-write path) is the F04 (P2-W8-F04) consumer-site responsibility.

**No actual embedding inference.** `EmbeddingChunker` produces `EmbeddingChunk` (text + metadata + token_count) — it does NOT call `OnnxSession::infer` or `CodeRankEmbed::embed`. F04 owns the embed-each-chunk pipeline; F05 ships the chunk producer only.

**No async API.** `EmbeddingChunker::chunk` is sync (`fn`, not `async fn`) — `tokenizers::Tokenizer::encode` and `ucil_treesitter::Parser::parse` are both sync. The natural async wrap is `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` at F04's consumer site.

**No sliding-window / overlap policy.** Master-plan §12.2 line 1339 prescribes 'signature + first-paragraph doc comment' for oversize functions — F05 honours this verbatim. Sliding-window splitting would be a future ADR-driven change (likely F04 or P3 if recall@10 metrics motivate it); F05 ships ONLY the master-plan-prescribed policy.

**No language-specific signature extraction beyond first-N-non-blank-lines heuristic.** The `ucil_treesitter::Chunk` struct does not surface `signature` separately (the data is computed inside `ucil_treesitter::chunker.rs`'s `extract_signature` private helper); re-extracting the same shape in F05 would either duplicate ts-chunker logic OR require an ADR-driven `Chunk::signature: Option<String>` schema bump on `ucil-treesitter`. F05 takes the pragmatic shortcut — first ≤3 non-blank lines as signature heuristic — and documents this in the rustdoc as a known limitation. A future ADR may motivate exposing `signature` from `ucil-treesitter::Chunk` — out of F05 scope.

**No interaction with WO-0057 (P2-W7-F06 search_code G2 fused).** WO-0057 modifies only `crates/ucil-daemon/{server.rs, plugin_manager.rs, lib.rs, g2_search.rs}` per its AC24 allow-list. WO-0060's allow-list (`crates/ucil-embeddings/**` only) is disjoint. The two WOs are safe to run in parallel; merge order does not matter.

**No new top-level workspace dep.** `ucil-treesitter` is a path dep on a sibling crate; declaration goes only in `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`, NOT in root `Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]`. `tokenizers`, `thiserror`, `ucil-core`, `tokio`, `tracing`, `ndarray`, `ort` are already available as workspace deps from prior WOs (WO-0058, WO-0059).

**No alignment of `ndarray 0.16` workspace dep with `ort`'s internal `0.17`** — that's WO-0058 lessons line 555 follow-up, deferred to F04 (LanceDB indexer) per WO-0059 lessons line 621. F05 does NOT touch `ndarray` directly (chunker uses `tokenizers` + `ucil-treesitter` + `std`).

**No new ADR.** DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC, ~300+ LOC for module-coherent NEW files), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait/struct boundaries — synthetic-tokenizer injection in tests is NOT a critical-dep mock), and the existing master-plan §12.2 line 1339 chunking policy all cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. `tokenizers` 0.x → 1.0 API rename, `ucil_treesitter::Parser::parse` returning a non-`Result` type that needs adapter), STOP and write an ADR before working around — purely upstream-fitting deviations may be documented inline per WO-0058 lessons line 543 (NOT in an ADR).

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` (M1, M2, M3 per AC22-AC24) are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 → WO-0058 → WO-0059, now 13 consecutive cleared excluding abandoned WO-0049 / WO-0050 / WO-0054).

**No edits to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/build_minimal_onnx.py`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/minimal.onnx`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`, `tests/integration/**`, `tests/fixtures/**`** — F05 is contained to NEW `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` + modify `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs` + modify `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` + auto-update `Cargo.lock` + NEW `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/sample.rs` + NEW `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F05.sh` + RFR marker.
**No `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

**No mock of `tokenizers::Tokenizer`, `ucil_treesitter::Parser`, `ucil_treesitter::Chunker`, `tree_sitter::Tree`.** The synthetic-tokenizer construction (`Tokenizer::new(WordLevel...)`) uses the real `tokenizers` crate API with a synthetic vocab — this is NOT a mock per DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait/struct boundaries) + WO-0048 lessons line 363 standing rule (synthetic test fixtures constructed via the real API are NOT critical-dep mocks). Verifier mock-detection grep MUST exclude `Tokenizer::new`/`WordLevelBuilder` patterns.
**No `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
acceptance: AC01 — `cargo build -p ucil-embeddings` exits 0.

AC02 — `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` declares `ucil-treesitter = { path = "../ucil-treesitter" }` — verified via `grep -cE '^ucil-treesitter = \{ path = "\.\./ucil-treesitter" \}' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` returning `1`.

AC03 — `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` exists and declares `pub struct EmbeddingChunker` — verified via `test -f crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs && grep -nE '^pub struct EmbeddingChunker' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs`.

AC04 — `cargo clippy -p ucil-embeddings --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0. Pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` returns ZERO unbacked uppercase tokens (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`).
AC05 — Frozen acceptance: `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture -- --nocapture` exits 0.

AC06 — Test selector `chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture` lives at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Verifier checks via `grep -nE '^fn test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` returning a column-0 match (no leading whitespace) AND NOT preceded by an open `mod tests {` brace.

AC07 — Sub-assertion 1 (chunk count positive): `chunks` is non-empty after running the chunker against `tests/data/sample.rs`. Operator-readable panic message MUST quote the actual `chunks` Debug-print on failure.

AC08 — Sub-assertion 2 (cap respected): every chunk's `token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` (= 512). Operator-readable panic MUST quote the offending chunk's full Debug-print + token_count + MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS values.

AC09 — Sub-assertion 3 (real tokenizer): `chunks[0].token_count` matches the synthetic tokenizer's whitespace-word-count over the first chunk's content (NOT the byte-estimate `max(1, ⌈len/4⌉)`). The expected count is computed in-test via `chunks[0].content.split_whitespace().count() as u32` and the assertion is `assert_eq!(chunks[0].token_count, expected, "token_count must reflect real tokenizer (whitespace word count); got {} expected {}", chunks[0].token_count, expected);`.

AC10 — Sub-assertion 4 (metadata preserved): `chunks[0].file_path == fixture_path`, `chunks[0].start_line >= 1`, `chunks[0].end_line >= chunks[0].start_line`, `!chunks[0].content.is_empty()`. Each assertion has an operator-readable panic message.
AC11 — Sub-assertion 5 (source order): `chunks` is sorted by `start_line` ascending. Operator-readable panic for any out-of-order pair.
AC12 — Unit test `from_tokenizer_path_returns_missing_tokenizer_file_for_absent_path` passes — exercises the `MissingTokenizerFile` error variant.
AC13 — Unit test `from_tokenizer_path_returns_tokenizer_for_invalid_json` passes — exercises the `Tokenizer { message }` error variant via 21 bytes of garbage in a tempfile.
AC14 — Unit test `retokenize_chunk_collapses_oversize_to_signature` passes — exercises the oversize-fallback branch via a synthetic 1000+-word `ucil_treesitter::Chunk` literal.
AC15 — Unit test `collapse_to_signature_handles_single_line_oversize_content` passes — exercises the byte-budget hard-truncation safety-net for single-line oversize content.
AC16 — Unit test `embedding_chunk_round_trips_metadata` passes — smoke test for `Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq` derives on `EmbeddingChunk`.
AC17 — Workspace-wide regression: `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exits 0. (Per WO-0058 / WO-0059 standing rule.)
AC18 — Phase-1 e2e regression: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` exits 0.

AC22 — Pre-baked mutation #1 (chunk() body neutered):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*pub fn chunk(&mut self, file_path: &Path/,/^[[:space:]]*}$/{ /^[[:space:]]*pub fn chunk/!{ /^[[:space:]]*}$/!d; }; }' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` — replaces the `chunk()` body with empty (returns `Ok(Vec::new())` if a default-return is implicit; otherwise causes compile error).  
  Runtime-only variant (use this if the literal sed produces a `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade per WO-0046 lessons line 245): replace the FIRST executable statement of `chunk()` with `let _ = (file_path, source, language); return Ok(Vec::new());` and add `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return, clippy::let_unit_value)]` on the function for the mutation step.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture`: panic at AC07's chunk-count assertion (`chunks.is_empty() == true` after running against sample.rs).  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs`. Rerun: green.

AC23 — Pre-baked mutation #2 (oversize-fallback neutered):  
  Runtime-only variant: locate the `if real_count > MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` branch in `retokenize_chunk` and replace its body with `// mutation: skip collapse_to_signature` + emit the chunk unchanged (i.e. `return Ok(EmbeddingChunk { ..., token_count: real_count });` even when `real_count > MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS`). Add `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return)]` on the function for the mutation step.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings chunker::retokenize_chunk_collapses_oversize_to_signature -- --nocapture`: panic at AC14's `token_count <= MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS` assertion (the synthetic 1000-word chunk leaks past the cap).  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs`. Rerun: green.

AC24 — Pre-baked mutation #3 (token_count uses byte-estimate instead of real):  
  Runtime-only variant: locate the `EmbeddingChunk { ..., token_count: real_count }` literal construction site in `retokenize_chunk` and replace `real_count` with `ast_chunk.token_count` (the byte-estimate from `ucil_treesitter::Chunk.token_count`). Add `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::needless_return)]` on the function if the local `real_count` becomes unused.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings chunker::test_embedding_chunker_real_fixture`: panic at AC09's `token_count == expected_word_count` assertion (the byte-estimate materially differs from the synthetic-tokenizer's word count for typical Rust source).  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs`. Rerun: green.

AC25 — Stub-scan: `rg -nE 'todo!\(\)|unimplemented!\(\)|panic!\(".*not yet|TODO|FIXME' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` returns ZERO hits. (`unwrap()` is permitted ONLY inside `#[cfg(test)]` per root CLAUDE.md.) The `EmbeddingChunker::chunk` body and all helpers contain REAL implementations, NOT stubs.
